text.title: Rust HTTP Handlers (Axum) Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real rust-http-handlers deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Handler signature takes / returns String; JSON shape drifts silently; no compile-time check.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped typed DTOs for 'quick endpoint'.
antipattern.fix: Typed DTOs with serde + validator; raw strings allowed only on debug routes.

antipattern.symptom: HTTP client built fresh inside every handler; connection pool never reused; latency explodes.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated client as a local variable.
antipattern.fix: Store clients in AppState; share via Arc.

antipattern.symptom: 500 response body contains SQL error / stack trace / internal IDs.
antipattern.root-cause: AppError IntoResponse passes display() directly to the body.
antipattern.fix: Scrub public message; full detail stays in tracing fields with correlation id.

antipattern.symptom: Tail latency triage requires manually correlating logs by timestamp.
antipattern.fix: Add instrument(skip(state)) to every handler; emit span fields for request id + entity id.
